title: Fix the Invalid Index Error in Array Slicer

statement.markdown: Implement a function `sliceArray` that takes an array `arr` and two integers `start` and `end` as input. It should return a new array containing the elements from the `start` index (inclusive) to the `end` index (exclusive). However, the function currently raises an error when the `start` index is greater than the `end` index. Fix the bug to handle this edge case correctly.
statement.outputFormat: Output format: A new array containing the sliced elements
statement.notes: Note that the input array `arr` is guaranteed to be non-empty.

examples.explanation: Slice from index 3 to 1 (exclusive) - should return an empty array

hints.text: Check the condition for swapping `start` and `end` indices

editorial.title: Editorial: Handling Invalid Index Error in Array Slicer
editorial.intuition: Swap `start` and `end` indices when `start` is greater than `end`
editorial.bruteForce: Simple if-else statement to swap indices
editorial.betterSolution: Use a conditional expression to swap indices
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a single line of code to swap indices
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Prove that swapping indices handles the edge case correctly
commonMistakes: Mistaking the condition for swapping indices
